project.name: letslive
project.description: LetsLive is an end-to-end livestreaming platform (Twitch-like) built as a Go-microservices backend (auth, user, livestream, vod, transcode, finance, chat) with a Next.js/React web client and a Flutter mobile app, using RTMP ingestion, FFmpeg HLS transcoding with adaptive bitrate, MinIO storage, Kong API gateway with Consul service discovery, and Grafana Loki/Tempo observability. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and high-level architecture diagrams to understand the project goal: build a Twitch-like livestreaming platform with RTMP ingest, HLS transcoding, and microservices backend.

tour.title: Composition & Deployment
tour.description: The Docker Compose files wire every microservice, Kong gateway, Postgres, MinIO, Consul, and Grafana stack. Kong config defines all public route mappings.

tour.title: RTMP Ingest & HLS Transcoding
tour.description: The Transcode service receives RTMP streams from OBS, spawns FFmpeg to produce adaptive HLS (360p/720p/1080p), and pushes segments to MinIO. Entry point and Dockerfile here.

tour.title: Livestream Metadata Service
tour.description: The Livestream Go service owns the live-room state, viewer counts, follower notifications, and chat history persistence.

tour.title: VOD Catalog
tour.description: The VOD service serves recorded streams, comments, and likes after a live session ends. Includes upload flow and comment subsystem.

tour.title: Auth & Users
tour.description: Auth issues JWTs and stores password hashes. User holds profiles, followers, notifications. Both services back the web account-setup and login flows.

tour.title: Finance / Wallet
tour.description: Finance service tracks wallet balances, deposit transactions, payments, and webhook callbacks from Stripe.

tour.title: Chat & Direct Messages
tour.description: Node.js chat server runs two WebSocket endpoints — broadcast room chat and direct messages — backed by Redis + Mongo. See chat commands spec for slash-command grammar.

tour.title: Shared Backend Library
tour.description: Cross-service Go middleware: correlation IDs, JWT validation, Consul service discovery, OTel tracing.

tour.title: Web Client — Next.js App
tour.description: Next.js 15 App Router with locale routing (en/vi), MSW for local dev, Sentry tracing. Auth pages live under (auth) route group, main app under (main).

tour.title: Web API & State
tour.description: API clients in web/lib/api wrap fetchers per backend service; web/hooks bundle SWR hooks per domain; web/contexts holds WS providers.

tour.title: Mobile App — Flutter
tour.description: Flutter app mirrors the web client features (auth, livestream, VOD, chat, wallet) using Riverpod + go_router + Dio. See setup review for current gap list.

tour.title: Observability
tour.description: Loki + Tempo + Promtail + OTel collector stack feeds Grafana for logs and traces. Configs live under configs/.

tour.title: Database Migrations
tour.description: Per-service Postgres migrations live under each backend/<svc>/migrations/. The docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-create-dbs.sql bootstraps separate databases per service.
